hubBlurb: Draft, summarize, extract action items, and ask questions across your workspace.
title: Learn Notion AI: Draft, Summarize & Ask Docs | Iro AI
h1: Make Notion AI draft, summarize, and answer for you.
metaDescription: How to use Notion AI: draft and summarize docs, pull action items from messy notes, autofill databases, and ask questions across your workspace.
lede: Notion AI is sitting right inside the notes, docs, and databases you already keep, but most people only use it to write a paragraph. It can pull action items out of a meeting, fill a database column across every row, and answer questions from your own pages. Iro teaches you the prompts that make it earn its place.
audience: Teams, founders, students, and anyone who runs their life in Notion
teaches: Drafting and editing docs with Notion AI

tldr.answer: Notion AI is the assistant built into Notion: it drafts and summarizes docs, pulls action items out of messy notes, autofills database properties, and answers questions across your whole workspace with Q&A. It's most useful when you give it real context to work from: a page of notes, a filled-in database, or a specific question about your own content. Type your goal and be explicit about the format you want back.
bullets: Trigger AI in any doc to draft, rewrite, summarize, or translate.
Turn raw meeting notes into an action-item list with owners and due dates.
Use AI autofill in databases and Q&A to search and synthesize across your workspace.

outcomesHeading: What you'll be able to do
outcomes: Draft a doc, then tighten its tone, length, and structure with AI in place
Summarize a wall of notes into decisions and clear action items
Autofill a database property (summaries, categories, next steps) across every row
Ask a question and get an answer sourced from your own pages
Build a wiki that stays useful instead of going stale

lessons.blurb: Prompt Notion AI for a first draft, then edit it into your voice right in the doc.
Turn raw meeting notes into a checklist with owners and due dates you can assign.
Get a tight summary at the top of a long doc, aimed at one specific reader.
Use AI autofill to categorize, summarize, or extract a field across every row.
Use Q&A to find and synthesize answers from your own pages, with links to the sources.

practice.scenario: You paste 20 lines of messy meeting notes into a Notion doc and want a clean list of next steps your team can actually act on.
practice.task: Choose the Notion AI prompt that turns the notes into something usable.

options.text: "From the notes above, extract every action item as a checklist. For each one, name the owner mentioned and any due date, and mark items with no owner as 'Unassigned'. Ignore general discussion that isn't a decision or a task."

practice.revealLabel: See why the second prompt wins
practice.feedback: The winning prompt is grounded in the notes above and asks for a specific output format (a checklist), names the exact fields to extract (owner and due date), handles an edge case (mark items with no owner as 'Unassigned'), and sets the scope (ignore general discussion). "Summarize these notes" gives you a paragraph you still have to read; "what should we do next?" asks the AI to decide for you rather than pull what your team already agreed. In Iro you'd write your own version and get feedback on the fields and edge cases you missed.

body.h2: Notion AI is context-hungry — feed it your pages
body.html:
Unlike a blank chatbot in another tab, Notion AI lives inside your content. It can read the doc you're in, pull from a database, and with Q&A search across your entire workspace (and connected tools like Slack or Google Drive on supported plans) to answer with links to the source pages. That means the quality of what you get out is tied to what you put in: a well-kept workspace makes Notion AI genuinely smart about your own work.

To trigger it, press space on an empty line or highlight text and choose Ask AI, then either pick an action (draft, summarize, improve, translate) or type your own instruction. As with any AI, the more precise you are about the format (a checklist, a three-bullet summary, a specific database field), the less editing you do afterward.

Extracting structured output from messy input — action items with owners, a summary for a named reader, a category for each row — is the same skill you'd use in ChatGPT, Claude, or Copilot. What is special about Notion AI is that your content is already there, so you don't paste anything, and Q&A can cite the exact page an answer came from. Learn the extract-to-format habit here and it pays off in every AI tool you touch.

faq.a: Notion AI is the AI assistant built into Notion. It drafts, summarizes, translates, and improves text, extracts action items, autofills database properties, and answers questions across your workspace with Q&A, pointing you to the source pages it used.

faq.q: How do I use Notion AI in a doc?
faq.a: Press the space bar on an empty line, or highlight text and choose Ask AI. Then pick a built-in action like draft, summarize, or improve, or type your own instruction. Being specific about the format you want back gives you a cleaner result.

faq.q: What is Notion AI database autofill?
faq.a: AI autofill is a database property that generates a value for every row from a prompt (a summary of the page, a category, a sentiment, or key info pulled from the content) and can update as the row changes, so you don't fill fields by hand.

faq.q: Can Notion AI answer questions about my workspace?
faq.a: Yes. Notion Q&A searches across your pages (and connected tools like Slack or Google Drive on supported plans) and answers your question with links to the sources, so you can verify where each part of the answer came from.

Notion AI is a paid feature. It is bundled into Notion's paid plans and offered as an add-on, usually with a limited number of free responses to try first. The prompting skill you learn here works the same in free AI tools too.
